zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard WARD於1540年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼時，他的父親Thomas WARD25歲，母親Emma Hare Parnell21歲。他於大約1560年與Lucy Agnes BALLARD在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1585年在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼享年45歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard WARD于1540年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼时，他的父亲Thomas WARD25岁，母亲Emma Hare Parnell21岁。他于大约1560年与Lucy Agnes BALLARD在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1585年在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼享年45岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾米德萊頓 切尼。

nameMeanings.name: WARD
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a watchman or guard, from Middle English ward ‘watchman, guard’ (Old English weard, used as both an agent noun and an abstract noun).
English: occupational name from Middle English warde ‘armed guard’ (Old English weard ‘watching, guarding’), with the same meaning as 1 above.

Irish: shortened form of McWard, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Bhaird ‘son of the poet’. The surname occurs throughout Ireland, where three different branches of the family are known as professional poets.

variants.name: Warshawsky
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Meyer, Ari, Hyman.

Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from the city of Warsaw (Polish Warszawa), which became the capital of Poland at the end of the 16th century, after the destruction of Kraków by fire.
Americanized form of Polish Warszawski, a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Wardman
meanings: English (Yorkshire): occupational name from Middle English wardman, either in the general sense ‘watchman, guard’ (compare Ward ) or in the administrative sense ‘representative of a ward within a borough’.

variants.name: Warde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Cormac, Donal.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard WARD was born in 1540, in Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England, his father, Thomas WARD, was 25 and his mother, Emma Hare Parnell, was 21. He married Lucy Agnes BALLARD about 1560, in Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. He died in 1585, in Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 45, and was buried in Middleton Cheney, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard WARDが1540年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアミッドレトン チェンエイで生まれた時，父Thomas WARDは25歳，母Emma Hare Parnellは21歳であった。約1560年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアミッドレトン チェンエイでLucy Agnes BALLARDと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1585年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアミッドレトン チェンエイで45歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアミッドレトン チェンエイにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard WARD(이)가 1540년 영국 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas WARD(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Emma Hare Parnell(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1560년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이에서 Lucy Agnes BALLARD(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1585년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 미들레턴 천이에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard WARD родился в 1540 г. в местности миддлетон чэни, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas WARD было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Emma Hare Parnell, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Lucy Agnes BALLARD около 1560 г. в местности миддлетон чэни, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер в 1585 г. в местности миддлетон чэни, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 45 лет и был похоронен в местности миддлетон чэни, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.
